# comparisons/ — Manual Cross-Validation Suite

R scripts that re-run app analyses through metafor and write annotated `results/results_*.txt` snapshots. Used to spot-check a specific effect type, CI method, or analysis path against R.

**This is a manual suite.** The automated regression pipeline is `scripts/diff_benchmarks.mjs` + `benchmark_reference.json` (generated by `scripts/generate.R`). Run these scripts when adding a new effect type or debugging a statistical result.

---

## Prerequisites

### R side

- **R ≥ 4.0**, **metafor ≥ 4.0** (version printed to stdout when `scripts/generate.R` runs via `packageVersion("metafor")`).
- Additional packages: `metadat`, `clubSandwich`.

```r
install.packages(c("metafor", "metadat", "clubSandwich"))
```

### Node side

The automated test suite (`node run_tests.mjs`) and benchmark diff tool (`node scripts/diff_benchmarks.mjs`) depend on dev packages declared in `package.json` at the repo root. Install once before running:

```bash
npm ci
```

This installs `jsdom` (plot smoke-test DOM provider) and `d3` (required by the plot harness). The `package-lock.json` is committed — `npm ci` is reproducible and requires no network access beyond initial setup.

Known formula divergences between the app and metafor are documented in each script's output under `=== KNOWN DIVERGENCES ===` and in [`../benchmark-data.md`](../benchmark-data.md).

---

## Coverage

| Script | Features validated | Default dataset |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------|
| `scripts/compare.R` | 39 effect types; τ² estimators (REML/DL/ML/HE/PM/SJ/HS/EB/GENQ/PMM/GENQM); CI methods (normal/KH/t/PL); MH and Peto fixed-effects | effect-dependent (see table below) |
| `scripts/compare_mv.R` | Multivariate MA (CS/Diag/UN); REML/ML; ρ ∈ {0.1, 0.5, 0.9} | `data/mv_berkey98.csv` |
| `scripts/compare_regression.R` | Meta-regression; subgroup with Q_between; location-scale; permutation test | `data/regression_data.csv` |
| `scripts/compare_robust.R` | RVE / cluster-robust SE (CR1); three-level rma.mv | `data/cluster_data.csv` |
| `scripts/compare_selection.R` | Vevea-Hedges step-function; half-normal/power/neg-exp/beta selection models; trim-fill; p-curve; p-uniform | `data/selection_data.csv` |
| `scripts/compare_influence.R` | Leave-one-out; influence diagnostics; Baujat; cumulative meta; BLUP | `data/smd_data.csv` |

### Effect types in compare.R

| Category | Types |
|----------|-------|
| Continuous (two-group) | `SMD`, `SMDH`, `MD` |
| Ratio / variability | `ROM`, `CVR`, `VR` |
| Binary (2×2) | `OR`, `RR`, `RD`, `AS`, `YUQ`, `YUY`, `PHI`, `RTET` |
| Correlation | `COR`, `UCOR`, `ZCOR`, `PCOR`, `ZPCOR`, `R2`, `ZR2` |
| Paired / single-group | `MD_paired`, `SMD_paired`, `SMCC`, `SMD1`, `SMD1H`, `MN`, `MNLN` |
| Proportion | `PR`, `PLN`, `PLO`, `PAS`, `PFT` |
| Reliability (Cronbach's α) | `ARAW`, `ABT`, `AHW` |
| Rates | `IR`, `IRR`, `IRD`, `IRSD` |
| Biserial / point-biserial | `RBIS`, `RPB` |
| Pre-computed | `GENERIC` |

Not covered: `HR` (no metafor `measure`; supply pre-computed `yi`/`vi` as `GENERIC`), `GOR` (app-only implementation, no metafor equivalent — see `benchmark-data.md`).

---

## Invocation

All scripts accept `--input` and `--output` overrides. Defaults are shown below.

```bash
# Effect type × method × CI
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare.R
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare.R --effect OR --method DL --ci normal
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare.R --effect SMDH --method PM --ci KH
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare.R --effect SMD --ci PL

# Multivariate
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_mv.R
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_mv.R --struct UN --method ML --rho 0.9

# Meta-regression / subgroup / location-scale / permutation
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_regression.R
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_regression.R --method DL --ci KH --perm 2000

# Cluster-robust / three-level
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_robust.R --mode rve
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_robust.R --mode 3l --method REML

# Selection models / trim-fill / p-curve / p-uniform
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_selection.R
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_selection.R --type vevea
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_selection.R --type trimfill

# Influence / LOO / Baujat / cumulative / BLUP
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_influence.R
Rscript comparisons/scripts/compare_influence.R --method DL
```

---

## Snapshot files

Output files follow the pattern `results_<SPEC>.txt`. They are committed alongside the scripts so reviewers can read expected values without running R.

| Script | Output file(s) |
|--------|----------------|
| `scripts/compare.R` | `results_<EFFECT>_<METHOD>_<CI>.txt` |
| `scripts/compare_mv.R` | `results_MV_<STRUCT>_<METHOD>_rho<RHO>.txt` |
| `scripts/compare_regression.R` | `results_REG_<METHOD>_<CI>.txt` |
| `scripts/compare_robust.R` | `results_RVE_<METHOD>.txt`, `results_3L_<METHOD>.txt` |
| `scripts/compare_selection.R` | `results/results_SELECTION.txt` (all) or `results_<TYPE>.txt` |
| `scripts/compare_influence.R` | `results/results_INFLUENCE.txt` |

---

## Known divergences

Each snapshot's `=== KNOWN DIVERGENCES ===` section lists expected differences between the app and metafor. Recurring ones:

- **Log-likelihood constants** — JS `evalLL` omits normalising constants (`-k/2·log(2π)`, `+½·log|X'X|`) that are constant in θ and irrelevant for optimisation. LRTs cancel the offset; raw LL values differ by a fixed constant documented per script.
- **VH-C one-sided selection model** — metafor caps `log(delta) ≤ 100`; JS BFGS is unconstrained. Only affects VH-C.
- **Beta selection model se_mu** — JS and R use different Hessian approximations at the same MLE. See `../memory/feedback_beta_selmodel.md`.
- **Cook's D** — metafor normalises by `(p+1)`; JS uses `W·Δ²` directly. Values differ by a factor of 2 for intercept-only models.
- **Externally vs internally studentized residuals** — metafor `rstudent` uses LOO variance; JS `stdResidual` uses full-model variance.
- **Permutation p-values** — PRNG-dependent (JS: mulberry32; R: Mersenne-Twister). Observed test statistics must match; p-values will differ.
- **GOR, HR** — no metafor equivalent; not covered.

For estimators with no metafor equivalent at all (`DLIT`, `HSk`, `SQGENQ`, `EBLUP`, `GOR`), see `../benchmark-data.md`.
